headline: A reported US government shutdown of Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 turned frontier AI access into the window's dominant story.
body: Multiple channels report that Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 were pulled three days after launch under US government order, attributed to export-control directives and a claimed jailbreak vulnerability, with the restriction extending into EU jurisdictions. This pushed government regulation, frontier AI, and competitive positioning to the top of the rotation, alongside framing that OpenAI is losing control as Microsoft, Apple, and Google promote their own LLMs. In parallel, an inference-speed surge broke out: Xiaomi and TileRT's MiMo-V2.5-Pro-UltraSpeed and Google DeepMind's DiffusionGemma both clear 1,000 tokens per second using quantization, speculative decoding, persistent GPU kernel runtime, and diffusion techniques that shift local models from memory-bound to compute-bound. New entrants speculative decoding, GPU kernel runtime, tokens per second, and compute-bound all surfaced around these releases.
